pi_items.source: Task description: 'strict carbon cap of 160 kg CO2 eq'. Verified through lifecycle assessment calculations using real USLCI transport data. Truck-only transport (2000 km, 1 tonne) yields ~221.8 kg CO2 eq, exceeding the limit. Rail+truck mix achieves target. Realistic and achievable.
Task description explicitly states: 'ship 1 ton of goods over 2000 km'. This distance value is stated consistently throughout the task specification.
Task description: 'Create a new process named freight_route_optimized'. This name is required and must be created by the agent within OpenLCA.
Task description: 'Create a parameter named rail_share (0.0 to 1.0) to represent the percentage of distance traveled by rail'. Explicitly required in the task specification.
Task description: 'Write the optimal share value... to a file at ~/LCA_Results/optimization_report.txt'. Path is consistent with OpenLCA user home directory structure ($HOME = /home/ga).

uslci_database.source: Real, publicly available U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database maintained by NREL and FLCAC (Federal LCA Commons)
uslci_database.data_type: Real production data - cradle-to-gate, gate-to-gate, and cradle-to-grave LCI datasets

lcia_methods.source: Real TRACI 2.1 method (Tool for the Reduction and Assessment of Chemical and other environmental impacts) published by U.S. EPA
lcia_methods.availability: Publicly available via Figshare (article 25782786) and EPA
lcia_methods.data_type: Real LCIA characterization factors for environmental impact assessment

truck_ghg_emissions.factor: ~110.9 g CO2e per tonne-km (160.1 g CO2e per ton-mile from USLCI/EPA data)
truck_ghg_emissions.calculation: 1 tonne × 2000 km × 110.9 g/tonne-km = 221,800 g = 221.8 kg CO2e

rail_ghg_emissions.factor: ~17 g CO2e per tonne-km (from lifecycle assessment research)
rail_ghg_emissions.source: ourworldindata.org, Sustamize analysis of freight transport

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real, publicly available LCA data: the USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database) from NREL/FLCAC containing production data for U.S. materials and processes, and the TRACI 2.1 method (EPA's Tool for the Reduction and Assessment of Chemical and other environmental impacts) containing verified characterization factors for environmental impact assessment. The target GWP limit of 160 kg CO2 eq is verified as realistic for a 1-tonne, 2000-km freight transport scenario: all-truck transport yields approximately 221.8 kg CO2 eq, exceeding the target, while the required optimization (finding minimum rail share of ~33%) is achievable within the stated limit using real transport emission factors. All metadata claims—process name, parameter name, distance value, GWP limit, and output file path—are explicitly stated in the task specification and are internally consistent.
